title: Premium Clinical Skincare Brand — Product-Shot Finishing, Logo Vectorization, Per-SKU Label Merge & Print-Ready Packaging Export
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief (db_i 274): a "Premium Clinical Skincare Brand – Logo, Label & Packaging Design" launch wanting a cohesive visual identity for the product line — brand logo, product label, product box/packaging, visual identity system, brand color palette & typography, AND explicitly "Print-ready files and editable source files." Stated aesthetic verbatim: clean/minimalist, modern/timeless, science-driven/trustworthy, premium-not-luxurious, clinical credibility, strong typography, whitespace, white-bg ecom. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: from-scratch logo/identity ART AND original layout COMPOSITION are [no]/[X] on the Adobe connector, so this task is scoped to the production & finishing slice the connector genuinely owns — the client supplies (a) a raw hero product photograph, (b) a flat raster of the chosen logo mark, (c) a lifestyle texture plate, and (d) a per-SKU label-copy CSV, plus pre-authored .ai label / .indd box templates carrying genuine merge fields (the one hand-authored step, per the data-merge guide). The connector then does all repeatable craft: clinical white-bg cleanup, neutral-tone grading, logo vectorization to editable SVG, type-system recommendation, packaging-safe wide-landscape reframe-cropping of the hero, a clinical monochrome variant, per-SKU label data-merge, print-ready packaging PDF export, and a presentation board. Concrete brand name "DERMAVERA", 3 SKUs (Serum / Cream / Cleanser), and exact specs are reasonable enrichment; nothing contradicts the brief, which only truncates mid-"Product Li[ne]".
one_line_ask: Take our raw skincare hero shot, flat logo mark, texture plate and SKU copy sheet and produce a clinically clean white-background product image set, an editable vector logo, a recommended type system, a packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the hero (cropped from the pure-white-sweep master), a clinical monochrome variant, per-SKU print labels merged from our label template, and a print-ready packaging PDF — all assembled on one presentation board.
full_brief: We're launching DERMAVERA, a premium clinically-tested skincare line, and need a cohesive, credible visual identity executed across the product line (3 launch SKUs: a Vitamin-C Serum, a Barrier Repair Cream, and a Gentle Gel Cleanser). The look must be clean, minimalist, modern, science-driven and trustworthy — premium without being flashy, professional without looking pharmaceutical. We are supplying: (1) one raw hero photograph of the Serum bottle shot on a near-white sweep (slightly tilted, mixed lighting, cluttered edges); (2) a flat high-res raster of our chosen logo mark (a minimal molecule/droplet monogram + "DERMAVERA" wordmark) that we need delivered as a clean editable vector; (3) a soft lifestyle texture plate (matte stone surface) for collateral grounding; (4) a label-copy spreadsheet with one row per SKU (name, claim, volume, INCI line, batch placeholder, hex accent). We also supply our pre-authored print templates: an Illustrator label template (label.ai) with genuine Variables, and an InDesign unit-carton template (carton.indd). Deliverables: a flawless clinical white-background hero image (straightened, background swept to pure white, neutral clinical color, crisp product), a 1:1 e-commerce crop and a packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the hero cropped to the target 5000×2800px (the pure-white-sweep master crop-and-resized to the wider ratio, centered on the product — sweep trimmed top/bottom, no canvas added), a clinical monochrome (single-accent duotone) brand variant of the hero, the logo delivered as an editable SVG, a recommended display+text type pairing, a print-ready per-SKU label set generated by merging our copy sheet into label.ai, a print-ready packaging PDF exported from carton.indd, and a single Firefly presentation board collecting the system for sign-off. Everything must be print-ready and leave us editable source (vector SVG + the authored .ai/.indd). Maintain consistent neutral, trustworthy color across every image.

inputs.name: dermavera_serum_hero_raw.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal product photograph of a single frosted-glass cosmetic serum bottle with a matte white pump cap, standing on a seamless near-white paper sweep, shot slightly tilted about 4 degrees off vertical, mixed warm-and-cool studio lighting causing a faint yellow-green color cast, soft shadow pooling at the base, a few dust specks and a stray lint thread on the sweep, slightly cluttered frame edges with a sliver of a softbox reflector visible top-left, realistic glass refraction and subtle fingerprints on the bottle, real micro-texture on the paper, candid product-test feel not a polished hero, 4000x5000px, documentary realism, no text on the label area (blank label panel)
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect: tilt to exercise auto_straighten, non-pure-white sweep + color cast to exercise remove_background/fill_area + auto_tone/color_temperature, blank label panel so it reads as a real un-branded raw shot. No CGI gloss; real dust, lint, fingerprints, soft real shadow.

inputs.name: dermavera_logo_mark_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A minimal flat clinical skincare logo on a pure white background: a thin-line geometric molecule-meets-droplet monogram glyph above the wordmark 'DERMAVERA' set in an elegant modern serif, all in a single deep slate-teal ink color (#16403B), perfectly crisp vector-like edges, generous whitespace, centered lockup, no gradients, no photographic texture, high resolution 3000x3000px, the kind of clean flat logo a brand hands off as a raster for vectorization
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for accurate legible wordmark text + crisp logo edges. Flat single-color on white = ideal clean input for image_vectorize → SVG. No drop shadows or gradients that would muddy the trace.

inputs.name: dermavera_stone_texture_plate.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat top-down photograph of a matte pale grey-beige stone / terrazzo surface, very fine natural speckle, soft even diffused light, no objects, edge-to-edge texture, subtle real grain, neutral cool-warm balance, 4000x2500px, suitable as a calm premium collateral background plate
inputs.realism_notes: Cheap texture generator is fine; this is a grounding plate for the monochrome collateral variant and the presentation board, not a hero. Neutral so the brand tint reads cleanly.

inputs.name: dermavera_sku_label_copy.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a 3-row CSV with header exactly: sku_name,claim,volume,inci_line,batch,accent_hex . Row1: 'Vitamin-C Brightening Serum','15% L-Ascorbic Acid — clinically tested','30 ml e','Aqua, Ascorbic Acid, Propylene Glycol, Ferulic Acid, Tocopherol','BATCH ####','16403B'. Row2: 'Barrier Repair Cream','Ceramide + Niacinamide complex','50 ml e','Aqua, Glycerin, Niacinamide, Ceramide NP, Squalane, Panthenol','BATCH ####','16403B'. Row3: 'Gentle Gel Cleanser','pH-balanced daily cleanse','150 ml e','Aqua, Coco-Glucoside, Glycerin, Sodium PCA, Allantoin','BATCH ####','16403B'. Column names MUST match the label.ai Variable names exactly.
inputs.realism_notes: Realistic cosmetic label fields (INCI, volume with 'e' estimated-sign, clinical claim, batch placeholder). Header names match the authored Illustrator Variables so document_merge_data_vector binds one label artboard per SKU row.

inputs.name: label.ai
inputs.gen_model: authored-template (desktop Illustrator, Variables panel) — NOT model-generated
inputs.gen_prompt: Hand-authored Illustrator label template, one artboard 70x90mm + 3mm bleed, CMYK. Static art: placed DERMAVERA vector logo (top-centre), thin hairline rule, clinical whitespace layout. Genuine Illustrator Variables (Object→Variables, bound as Dynamic objects NOT literal text): {sku_name} (display heading), {claim} (subhead), {volume} (lower-right), {inci_line} (small-print body block), {batch} (footer micro-text). One swatch/text accent driven by {accent_hex}. CSV headers sku_name,claim,volume,inci_line,batch,accent_hex match the Variable names exactly so document_merge_data_vector emits one label per row.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the single hand-authored step the capability sheet allows for data-merge [T] — listed as a supplied authored input asset, not produced by the connector. Without genuine Variables nothing binds.

inputs.name: carton.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: Hand-authored InDesign unit-carton dieline template, single document with the 5 box panels laid out flat (front / back / 2 sides / top) on a carton dieline, 3mm bleed, CMYK, registration + crop marks. Front panel has a named picture frame 'HERO_PLATE' sized to receive the finished white-bg hero plate, a placed DERMAVERA vector logo, and live text frames for the SKU name; back panel has an INCI/claims text area and a reserved barcode rectangle. No data-merge — this is a layout to EXPORT via document_render_layout to a print-ready PDF with the finished hero plate placed.
inputs.realism_notes: Authored .indd supplied so document_render_layout can EXPORT it (export-only, never composes). The named 'HERO_PLATE' frame is pre-sized to the finished hero from the photo chain.

outputs.name: dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.png
outputs.spec: Finished clinical hero: auto-straightened, background swept to pure #FFFFFF, neutral-graded (auto-tone + neutralized color temp + lifted highlights), crisp product. Full-res ~4000x5000px PNG, sRGB, e-commerce white-bg standard.

outputs.name: dermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.png
outputs.spec: 1:1 square e-commerce crop of the finished hero, product centered with clinical whitespace, 2048x2048px PNG on pure white.

outputs.name: dermavera_hero_packaging_wide.png
outputs.spec: Packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the finished hero: the pure-white-sweep hero is crop-and-resized to a 5000x2800px landscape ratio centered on the product (portrait frame trimmed top/bottom to hit the wider ratio — no canvas added, no pixels invented). Because the source sweep is already pure #FFFFFF, the reframed landscape reads as a clean white field for carton/hero-plate placement. ~5000x2800px PNG, sRGB.

outputs.name: dermavera_hero_mono_variant.png
outputs.spec: Clinical monochrome brand variant of the finished hero — single deep slate-teal (#16403B) duotone/monochromatic tint, for collateral. Same resolution as hero, PNG.

outputs.name: dermavera_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Editable vector SVG of the supplied logo mark, clean single-color paths, transparent background — the editable source the brief demands.

outputs.name: dermavera_type_system.json
outputs.spec: Recommended display + text typeface pairing (PostScript names + rationale) from font_recommend, to anchor the visual-identity typography spec.

outputs.name: dermavera_labels_merged.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready per-SKU label set: 3 label artboards (one per SKU row) produced by merging the copy CSV into label.ai. CMYK, 3mm bleed, crop marks. Vector + editable source = label.ai retained.

outputs.name: dermavera_carton_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready unit-carton PDF exported from carton.indd with the finished white-bg hero plate placed in HERO_PLATE, CMYK, 3mm bleed, registration/crop marks.

outputs.name: dermavera_brand_board_deeplink
outputs.spec: Firefly Board deep-link assembling the finished hero, 1:1 crop, wide canvas, mono variant, vector logo and label proofs into one sign-off presentation board.

connector_workflow.note: Begin upload of the client-supplied raw hero, logo raster, stone texture plate and the SKU copy CSV into CC storage. Initial asset-load step (no prior output to consume).
Finalize the multipart upload; returns asset URNs/presigned refs for dermavera_serum_hero_raw.png, dermavera_logo_mark_flat.png, dermavera_stone_texture_plate.jpg, dermavera_sku_label_copy.csv.
Level the ~4-degree tilt in the raw serum hero so the bottle stands true for clinical ecom.
Strip the off-white/cast sweep + cluttered edges + lint/dust → transparent PNG isolating the bottle.
Select the product subject to build a mask for the inverse (the background field).
Invert subject mask → background-only selection for a pure-white sweep fill.
Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white #FFFFFF (solid-color fill only) → clean clinical white-bg sweep behind the product.
Baseline neutral tonal balance on the white-bg hero.
Neutralize the residual warm/cool cast for trustworthy clinical color consistency across the line.
Lift/clean highlights so the white sweep reads as paper-white and glass stays crisp → FINAL clinical hero.
Produce the 1:1 2048x2048 e-commerce crop with centered product + whitespace.

Reframe the finished clinical hero to the packaging-safe wide 5000x2800px landscape target via an honest crop-and-resize centered on the product — the portrait frame is cropped top/bottom to hit the wider ratio, so the sweep above/below the bottle is trimmed (no canvas is added, no pixels invented). Because the source is already a pure-white sweep, the reframed landscape reads as a clean white field for carton/hero-plate placement.
Apply the single deep slate-teal #16403B monochromatic tint → clinical mono brand variant for collateral.
Trace the flat logo raster → clean editable single-color SVG (the editable-source deliverable).
Recommend the display + text typeface pairing for the clinical/premium identity (informed by the vectorized wordmark style); anchors the typography spec.
[T] Merge the SKU copy CSV into the authored label.ai (genuine Variables) → 3 print-ready per-SKU label artboards, CMYK + bleed. CSV headers match Variable names.
EXPORT the authored carton.indd (with the finished wide hero plate placed in the named HERO_PLATE frame) → print-ready packaging PDF, CMYK + registration/crop marks.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical, dermavera_hero_1x1_ecom, dermavera_hero_packaging_wide, dermavera_hero_mono_variant, dermavera_logo.svg, dermavera_labels_merged.pdf
connector_workflow.note: Assemble all produced asset URNs into one Firefly Board sign-off presentation (presents assets already produced; does not generate art).

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-2 load the client assets (the only steps with no prior input). The hero photo then flows linearly: straighten(3)→remove_bg(4)→select_subject(5)→invert(6)→fill white(7, consumes hero_nobg + bg_mask)→auto_tone(8)→color_temp(9)→highlights(10)=FINAL clinical hero. That final hero fans out into three derivatives — 1:1 crop(11), packaging-wide landscape reframe-crop(12, honest crop-and-resize to 5000x2800 centered on the product, no canvas added), mono variant(13) — each consuming dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical. In parallel the logo raster from loaded_assets is vectorized(14) and the type system(15) is recommended. The authored label.ai + SKU CSV (from loaded_assets) merge into per-SKU labels(16). carton.indd consumes the packaging-wide hero (step 12 output) placed in its HERO_PLATE frame and is exported(17). Finally the Firefly board(18) consumes six produced outputs (hero, 1:1, wide, mono, logo SVG, label PDF) to present the system. Every step after the initial load consumes at least one prior step's named output; the deliverable set emerges entirely from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, all server="adobe", 17 DISTINCT Adobe tools (image_crop_and_resize repeats at steps 11 and 12) — well past the ≥10 / ≥6 thresholds — spanning four tool groups (asset upload, the image_* raster/tonal/vector family, document_* merge+export, and create_firefly_board). It exercises a non-trivial masking sub-chain (remove_bg → select_subject → invert → solid fill_area) for a genuine clinical white-bg result, an honest crop-only reframe to the wide packaging ratio (no canvas extension), a [T] authored-template data-merge, and an export-only [C] layout render — forcing the agent to respect the exact capability boundaries (no gen-fill, no compositing, fill_area=solid only, render_layout=export only, merge needs authored Variables). Difficulty also comes from honoring grounding: the from-scratch logo/identity ART and original layout COMPOSITION in the source brief are out of scope, so the agent must correctly re-scope to the production/finishing slice while still delivering print-ready files + editable source as the brief demands. Mostly [C] with a single [T] step, no widget/async stalls — runnable end-to-end in the product.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned generation capability remains in the workflow. Step 12 now uses image_crop_and_resize — an HONEST landscape reframe of the pure-white-sweep hero to 5000x2800px, centered on the product with the portrait frame trimmed top/bottom to hit the wider ratio (no canvas added, no white field padded on, no pixels invented). This replaces the earlier image_fill_area wording, which incorrectly implied a portrait→wider-landscape canvas extension that image_fill_area (solid-color region fill only) cannot perform headless. Full scan confirms no outpaint/generative-expand, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, and no object-removal-with-reconstruction. All connector actions map to real, non-generative Adobe tools: image_auto_straighten, image_remove_background (mask), image_select_subject/invert_selection (mask), image_fill_area (solid fill, step 7 only), image_apply_auto_tone/adjust_color_temperature/adjust_highlights (grade), image_crop_and_resize (crop, steps 11 & 12), image_apply_monochromatic_tint (grade), image_vectorize (trace), font_recommend, document_merge_data_vector ([T] authored-template merge), document_render_layout (export-only), create_firefly_board (assembly). Canvas-extension / 'placed on a solid pure-white field' phrasing has been reworded to the honest crop-reframe method in full_brief, one_line_ask, grounding_note, outputs[].spec and chaining_note, and the difficulty_rationale distinct-tool count corrected to 17.
reverify.notes: Step 12 tool changed from image_fill_area to image_crop_and_resize to eliminate the canvas-extension implication (QA: CANVAS-extension). distinct_adobe_tools stays 17 and tool_call_count stays 18 — image_fill_area now appears once (step 7) and image_crop_and_resize repeats (steps 11 & 12). difficulty_rationale corrected from '18 DISTINCT' to '17 DISTINCT'. The pre-existing render_layout export-only note on step 17 is out of scope for this drift fix and left as-is.
